---
title: "2.2.4 Automatic Repeat Requests (ARQs)"
subtitle: "Data Transmission P62"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)

## Automatic Repeat Request (ARQ)

### A third way to check data

<span class="term">ARQ (Automatic Repeat Request)</span> (自动重复请求) is a third way used to check data following data transmission (after parity checks and echo checks).

ARQ uses <span class="mark">positive and negative acknowledgements</span> and <span class="mark">timeout</span>:

::: {.tight-list}
- The receiving device receives an <span class="mark">error detection code</span> as part of the data transmission (this is typically a <span class="term">Cyclic Redundancy Check — CRC</span>)
- This is used to detect whether the received data contains any transmission errors
- If <span class="mark">no error is detected</span>, a <span class="mark">positive acknowledgement</span> is sent back to the sending device
- If <span class="mark">an error is detected</span>, the receiving device sends a <span class="mark">negative acknowledgement</span> and requests <span class="mark">re-transmission of the data</span>
:::

## ARQ: Timeout and Retransmission

### Handling the case when no acknowledgement arrives

::: {.tight-list}
- A <span class="term">timeout</span> (超时) is used by the sending device — it waits a <span class="mark">pre-determined amount of time</span>
- If <span class="mark">no acknowledgement</span> of any type has been received within this time limit, the sender <span class="mark">automatically re-sends the data</span>
- This continues until a <span class="mark">positive acknowledgement is received</span>
- Or until a <span class="mark">pre-determined number of re-transmissions</span> has taken place
- ARQ is often used by <span class="mark">mobile phone networks</span> to guarantee data integrity
:::

## ARQ Process Summary

### Complete ARQ flow

::: {.tight-list}
1. Sender transmits data with error detection code (CRC)
2. Receiver checks for errors using the error detection code
3. If <span class="mark">no error</span>: receiver sends <span class="mark">positive acknowledgement</span> back to sender
4. If <span class="mark">error detected</span>: receiver sends <span class="mark">negative acknowledgement</span> and requests re-transmission
5. If <span class="mark">no acknowledgement received</span> within timeout period: sender <span class="mark">automatically re-sends</span> the data
6. Process repeats until positive acknowledgement or <span class="mark">pre-determined re-transmission limit</span> reached
:::

## Classroom Check

### Understand ARQ and retransmission

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="mark">ARQ uses positive/negative acknowledgements and timeout</span>
- receiver uses <span class="mark">error detection code (CRC)</span> to check for transmission errors
- <span class="mark">no error → positive acknowledgement</span> sent to sender
- <span class="mark">error detected → negative acknowledgement</span> and request for re-transmission
- <span class="mark">timeout: if no acknowledgement received</span> within time limit, sender automatically re-sends
- continues until <span class="mark">positive acknowledgement or pre-determined re-transmission limit</span>
- ARQ is used by <span class="mark">mobile phone networks</span> to guarantee data integrity
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Encryption

The next deck covers the purpose of encryption for protecting data privacy.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)
